# Todo

This file uses [Prose form](AGENTS.md#prose-form). It
contains near term tasks with a short description and
uses links or reference links for more details.

## In Progress

When a `## Todo` item is picked up, its text moves here: the
problem overview and its list of things to do. That is followed
by the "plan" — a bullet + sub-bullets list of the development
"ladder". Each step starts with a `[[N]]` reference slot —
replaced with the step's commit ref on first backfill, once
the step is pushed — then the version, the title, and a
`(done|current)` marker as appropriate:
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-0 blah (current)
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-1 blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y-2 blah blah blah
   - [[N]] 0.xx.y close-out and validation

At close-out the ladder lives on as the cycle's chores
`### As-built ladder`, where the refs are preserved
(renumbered to that file's local `# References` slots).

**feat: no_std band report modules**

The band-table capability lives in the demo, not the crate:
`FENCES`, `build_bands`, `print_table`, `commas` — ~200 of
`examples/h2demo.rs`'s 288 lines — while `src/` computes no band,
mean, or stdev. The same accumulate-then-render loop exists three
more times in `../iiac-perf` (`harness.rs`, `band_table.rs`,
`probe.rs`). Separately `SplitMix64`, the heavy-tailed stream, and
the `(7, 30)` config are copied verbatim across `tests/oracle.rs`,
`benches/record.rs`, and `examples/h2demo.rs`, so a change to one
silently diverges from the others.

Everything practical stays `no_std`: the new modules build report
*structures*; only writing them to stdout is `std`-gated. This
reverses ARCHITECTURE.md's "band-table reporting stays in tprobe"
decision — see [[5]].

- [[N]] 0.1.3-0 chore: band report cycle setup (current)
  - version-of-record, this ladder, chores section, the
    ARCHITECTURE.md reversal, and the iiac-perf adoption todo
- [[N]] 0.1.3-1 refactor: dev module for test, bench, demo
  - `dev/{mod,consts,rng,stream}.rs` behind one `#[path]`
    include per consumer; retires 3 `SplitMix64` copies, 3
    stream copies, and the scattered `(7, 30)` / seed / sigfig
    literals
  - folds in the `src/` unit-test literals: each
    `Config::new(g, n)` + hand-computed `[C; N]` pair becomes
    `const C` + `const N: usize = C.total_buckets()`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-2 feat: no_std band ladder and labels
  - `src/bands.rs`: `Boundary`/`BoundaryKind`, const ladder from
    `Z_DEPTH`/`N_DEPTH`, `BandLabels` with alloc-free label
    writing; fences as integer rationals, so no `floor`/`powi`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-3 feat: band assignment trait
  - `BandAssign` with `RankSplit` (h2demo's exact rank split)
    and `MidRank` (iiac-perf's right-closed Hazen mid-rank);
    tests pin a case where the two legitimately disagree
- [[N]] 0.1.3-4 feat: midpoint-weighted mean and variance
  - `src/stats.rs`: two-pass variance, avoiding the
    `sumsq/n - mean²` cancellation the demo has today; `stdev()`
    where a `sqrt` exists
- [[N]] 0.1.3-5 feat: band table structure
  - `src/table.rs`: fixed-capacity `BandTable` built in one pass
    over `Buckets`
- [[N]] 0.1.3-6 feat: band table rendering
  - `src/report.rs`: renders into any `core::fmt::Write` with
    two-pass width measurement; `std` adds the stdout convenience
- [[N]] 0.1.3-7 refactor: h2demo on library report path
  - demo ~288 → ~60 lines, gated on output identical to today's
    at matching ladder depths
- [[N]] 0.1.3-8 docs: band report modules
  - README, ARCHITECTURE, and the switch to the fuller z4..n10
    ladder as a visible change
- [[N]] 0.1.3 feat: no_std band report modules
  - close-out and validation

## Todo

 Entries are in **strict priority rank** — #1 highest,
 descending. Reprioritize by moving an entry, then
 `vc-x1 fix-todo --no-dry-run TODO.md` to renumber.
 The numbers are positional rank, not stable IDs — to refer
 to a Todo, name it by its **title** (a greppable mention;
 a numbered list item has no anchor to link to), not its
 number. Long-tail entries
 live in [todo-backlog.md](notes/todo-backlog.md). Use the
 [Prose Form in AGENTS.md](AGENTS.md#prose-form); deeper
 detail goes in `notes/chores/chores-NN.md` design
 subsections (link via `[N]` ref).

1. **Buffer-swap servicing model.** Today a probe has one
   histogram recorded to completion. Future: a 1-bit
   "needs service soon" signal per probe; a background task
   hands over a fresh zeroed buffer and takes the full one

   `examples/tp_pc`, then adopt in its core recording path.

content.content:
vc-x1 0.74.0-0

Commit both repos, push the work repo's BOOKMARK, and
squash-push the bot repo's `main` — one resumable command.

Collapses the manual commit-push-publish ceremony into a
single subcommand with two interactive approval gates and a
state machine with persistent progress so interruptions can
resume without re-doing completed stages.

Stages: preflight (tracking / bot-published / sync checks —
no build steps; run project checks yourself before pushing)
→ review (approve diff)
→ message ($EDITOR / --title+--body, approve text) →
commit-work → commit-bot (skipped if clean) → bookmark-set
(work repo → <bookmark>, bot repo → main) → push-work →
squash-push-bot. Failures in commit-work / commit-bot /
bookmark-set roll both repos back via
`jj op restore` to the snapshot recorded before commit-work.
After push-work succeeds the remote boundary is crossed and
recovery is forward-only.

Non-interactive use: pass both --title and --body plus --yes
to skip the review gate. Saved state carries title/body
across resumes so only the first invocation needs them.

Usage: vc-x1 push [OPTIONS] [BOOKMARK]

Arguments:
  [BOOKMARK]
          Bookmark to advance in the work repo (positional form of
          `--bookmark`). The bot repo always advances `main`.
          
          Accepting a positional lets the common case read as `vc-x1 push main`
          without the `--bookmark` ceremony; `--bookmark` is kept as an alias
          for scripts and for composition with other args. The two forms
          conflict if both supplied.

Options:
      --bookmark <BOOKMARK>
          Bookmark to advance in the work repo (flag form; see positional)

  -V, --version
          Print the `vc-x1 X.Y.Z` banner as the first line, then continue. With
          no subcommand, prints the banner and exits.
          
          Replaces clap's auto-version (which would exit after printing): the
          banner now rides along with normal subcommand execution rather than
          gating it, so scripts can capture the version *and* the command's
          output in one invocation.

      --restart
          Clear any saved state file and start from stage 1

  -v, --verbose...
          Verbose output: -v debug, -vv trace

      --from <STAGE>
          Explicit stage to jump to (advanced / debug use)

content.content:
Possible values:
          - preflight:       Verify bookmark tracking, the bot-published
            invariant, and sync state
          - review:          Present diff for the first approval gate
          - message:         Compose / edit the commit message; present for
            second gate
          - commit-work:     Commit the work repo
          - commit-bot:      Commit the `.claude` bot repo (skipped if empty)
          - bookmark-set:    Advance each repo's bookmark to its `@-`: work →
            `<bookmark>`, bot → `main`
          - push-work:       `jj git push --bookmark <b> -R .`
          - squash-push-bot: In-process squash of `.claude`'s trailing session
            writes + push `main`

      --log <LOG>
          Log file path (captures all levels)

      --step
          Pause between every stage for an interactive approval gate

      --status
          Print where the saved state thinks we are and exit

      --recheck
          Re-run `preflight` even on resume (default: skip if last run
          succeeded)

      --no-squash-push
          Stop before `squash-push-bot` so it can be run manually

      --dry-run
          Print the exact commands for every stage without side effects

      --title <STR>
          Commit title (skip `$EDITOR` for the message stage)

      --body <STR>
          Commit body (skip `$EDITOR` for the message stage)

  -y, --yes
          Auto-approve interactive prompts (review gate, message-edit
          confirmation). Required when `--title` / `--body` aren't both supplied

Pushing now with the approved title and body — `vc-x1 push` handles both repos and injects the `ochid:` trailers each way. Since the review and description gates already happened here in conversation, I'm passing `--title`/`--body`/`--yes` so it doesn't block on prompts I can't answer.

If preflight rejects the brand-new untracked bookmark, it rolls both repos back before any remote write, and I'll report that rather than retry.

vc-x1 push refactor-common-modules --title 'chore: band report cycle setup' --body 'Preparation for the band report cycle: promote the band-table
capability out of examples/h2demo.rs into no_std crate modules,
and collapse the dev-side duplication across test, bench, and
demo. Sets the version-of-record, the ladder, and the chores
section, and records the founding decision this reverses.

- Cargo.toml: version-of-record 0.1.2 -> 0.1.3-0
- Cargo.lock: version bump propagated
- TODO.md
  - In Progress: the cycle block — problem statement (band
    math stranded in the demo, four unshared copies of the
    accumulate-then-render loop, duplicated dev literals) and
    the ten-rung ladder, -0 marked done
  - new Todo #3 "iiac-perf adoption", peer to the tprobe
    entry; ref [5] to the new chores section
- ARCHITECTURE.md
  - feature map: reverses "band-table reporting stays in
    tprobe" — reporting is in-crate and no_std, building
    report structures, with only stdout behind std
  - readout requirements: records that the bar is now met
    here, and that the duplication ran opposite to what the
    original decision assumed
  - mean/stdev: core has no sqrt, so the structure carries
    variance and stdev() is offered where a sqrt exists;
    variance accumulated two-pass rather than sumsq/n - mean^2
  - drops a stray empty duplicate "## Validation" heading
- notes/chores/chores-01.md
  - opens "## feat: no_std band report modules" with an empty
    Commits: line, the intro, three design subsections
    (reporting moves in-crate, no_std constraints, the numeric
    fix), and an empty As-built ladder for the Work commits
  - backfills Commits: [[12]] on "perf: record path inlining,
    read-time total", now permanent on main; adds the [12]
    reference (9a79e0eb1922)' --yes 2>&1 | tail -30
